commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them. The blind see Christ shows them who he was by the miracles, which were foretold concerning the Messias. The poor have the gospel preached to them. This is the sense held forth by the prophet Isaias. 1) (Witham) That is, they are declared to have the kingdom of heaven, and are styled blessed. Here also he fulfils the prophecy of Isaias, (Chap. ) which in the Septuagint version is rendered, He sent me to preach the gospel to the poor. (Nicholas of Lyra.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Knowing therefore, as being God, the mind with which John had sent them, He straightway cured blind, lame, and many others; not to teach him (for how should He him that was convinced), but these that were doubting: and having healed them.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
